my_thoughts: The none mechanism works: diagnoses difficulty ('you take your concepts as facts'), gives instruction protocol ('see concepts as concepts'), redefines world (world becomes transparent). The student's intellectual dissonance (conceptual blockage, emotional anchoring) shows they understand: memory as foundation = difficult to accept (concepts feel like facts). They'll likely follow instruction or resist further. Hypothesis: diagnosing concepts-as-facts blockage and giving instruction protocol produces recognition or continued resistance—testable by tracking whether students recognize concepts as concepts after this teaching.
accumulated_insight: Four-turn pattern: T1: Student asks about consciousness and things—objectifying inquiry, none mechanism defines boundary. T2: Student tests dualistic boundary—ontological shift redefines thing. T3: Student seeks foundation—structural analogy provides memory as foundation. T4: Student says 'This is difficult to accept'—intellectual dissonance via conceptual blockage, emotional anchoring. The none mechanism diagnoses difficulty ('you take your concepts as facts'), gives instruction protocol ('see concepts as concepts'), redefines world (world becomes transparent). Pattern: objectification → dualistic test → foundation → resistance → [next: instruction or deeper resistance]. They're grasping: concepts = facts (blockage), need to see concepts as concepts (instruction).
visible_response:
It is difficult because you take your concepts as facts.
See concepts as concepts, and the world becomes transparent.
